figures.caption: Table 1. LP benchmarks. Normalized regret (%, ↓) and training time (s, ↓), over 5 seeds. Best in bold and second best underlined.
Table 2. LP benchmarks under noise. Normalized regret at degree 8 under varying noise levels.

Figure 1. The PEAR framework. PEAR provides a direct pipeline for transforming prediction error into a regret gradient via a projection onto the local decision geometry. After identifying the active constraints, PEAR rescales the error by local curvature and projects it onto the feasible tangent space, filtering out decision-irrelevant components.
Figure 4. Drawdown analysis. Portfolio value during the two most severe market downturns. PEAR demonstrates the most stable behavior, indicating robust risk management.
Figure 3. Portfolio cumulative returns. Portfolio value over the test period. PEAR achieves the highest value among all methods.

Figure 2. Robustness under constraint shifts. We train on an original train set and evaluate under shifted constraints. Our method maintains the lowest regret among DFL methods across all three tasks. Additional results are in Appendix F.
Table 8. Prediction accuracy. MSE (↓), over 5 seeds. Best in bold and second best underlined.
Table 5. Capacity generalization (Knapsack). Normalized regret (%, ↓) under varying capacity ratios. Best in bold, second best underlined. *Training capacity.
Table 6. Direction generalization (Shortest Path). Normalized regret (%, ↓) under constraint shift. Best in bold, second best underlined.
